steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls after 'poetic ____' and before a list of three noun phrases (terse compressed lines; syncopated rhythms; keen attention to everyday life). The punctuation must introduce that list after a complete independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘style. Terse,’: the period ends the sentence, leaving 'Terse, compressed lines …' as a fragment with no main verb. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘style; terse,’: a semicolon must join two independent clauses, but 'terse, compressed lines' is a noun phrase, not a clause. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘style: terse,’: the colon follows the complete independent clause 'the poem contains three signature elements of Brooks's poetic style' and correctly introduces the three-item list. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘style, terse,’: a single comma is not the right punctuation to introduce this list after an independent clause; a colon is needed. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
